title: PM Cram glossary term
description: A glossary term. Glossary terms double as flashcards in the app: the Term is the card front, Core Meaning / Expansion is the back. Field names and order follow the established generation prompt. STRICT JSON — never place a double-quote inside a string value; use single quotes for any inner quoting.

Term.description: The glossary term. Natural key used to dedupe across files (case-insensitive).

Expansion.description: Atomic ~2 sentences; Standard 3-5; Foundational a tight 3-4 sentence summary. Do not pad to length.

Deep Dive.description: null unless Depth is Foundational. If Foundational: 2-4 short paragraphs (what it is -> why it matters / where it applies -> the most common misconception -> how it shows up in real projects), separated by \n\n.

Contrast.description: The most commonly confused term + the distinction, in one line.

Figure.description: OPTIONAL explanatory diagram, shown on the flashcard back and term page. Same model as guide/deck figures.

svg.description: Inline theme-aware <svg> markup (use currentColor for ink).

image.description: Repo-relative path or absolute URL of a raster image.

Synonyms.description: OPTIONAL alternative names for the SAME concept (e.g. 'RACI chart', 'RACI matrix' for RACI). Searchable and resolve to this card; they must NOT also exist as their own terms.

Source.description: Cite the specific PMI standard + term (and page where known). Use 'not a formal PMI glossary entry' ONLY for Practitioner-tier terms. A Core glossary term must have a real PMI source. Write the standard name and term plainly, with no quotation marks around the term.
